behavior_summary: U8P_PLAQUENS::look handles the player examining the plaque: it emits a short descriptive bark ('plaque ') and then yields/suspends until the U8P_PLAQUENS::look interaction completes.
U8P_PLAQUENS::use is the item 'U8P_PLAQUENS::use' entry point and immediately delegates behavior to U8P_PLAQUENS::func00C2.

U8P_PLAQUENS::func00C2 queries the plaque instance's quality code (Item::getQuality(this)), uses that numeric value as a selector across many cases, and for each case starts a Plaque::read process with a specific hardcoded inscription string and a display flag (0x10). After spawning the read, the script captures the pid and suspends—effectively waiting for the displayed inscription to finish.
notable_control_flow: A simple examine path (U8P_PLAQUENS::look) that only shows a short bark and suspends.
A dispatcher path (U8P_PLAQUENS::func00C2) that performs a multi-way branch on the item's quality value to select one of many hardcoded inscription strings.
Spawn + suspend pattern: U8P_PLAQUENS::func00C2 starts Plaque::read with the selected string, captures the returned pid, then suspends to wait for the read/display to finish before returning.
open_questions: What exact range and meaning of quality codes (Item::getQuality) map to each inscription string? The file shows many cases but the numeric mapping detail is not reconstructed here.

Do any inscription cases set or depend on broader game state (global flags) beyond the local spawn/suspend behavior? The script shows no global flag reads/writes, but external gameplay systems might be involved.
Is the display flag (0x10) used uniformly for all Plaque::read calls significant (e.g., text formatting or voice) and does it interact with other UI systems?

main_function.name: U8P_PLAQUENS::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short examine/description text for the plaque and end the U8P_PLAQUENS::look interaction.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player 'looks at' or examines the plaque (U8P_PLAQUENS::look/inspect event).
No parameters or global flags required.

function_map.name: U8P_PLAQUENS::look
function_map.role: player-facing examine/description handler (emits a short bark and suspends).

function_map.name: U8P_PLAQUENS::use
function_map.role: U8P_PLAQUENS::use-entry process that delegates U8P_PLAQUENS::use behavior to the dispatcher U8P_PLAQUENS::func00C2.

function_map.name: U8P_PLAQUENS::func00C2
function_map.role: quality-to-inscription dispatcher: reads Item::getQuality(this), maps value to a literal inscription string and spawns Plaque::read with that string, then suspends.

internal_calls.purpose: Delegate the 'U8P_PLAQUENS::use' action to the dispatcher that selects and shows the appropriate inscription.

external_calls.purpose: Query the plaque item's numeric 'quality' or variant code used as the selector for which inscription to show.
Start/display the selected inscription text (passed a string pointer, display flag 0x10, and the plaque instance); the spawn returns a pid captured locally and the script suspends waiting for it.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_PLAQUENS::look handles the player examining the plaque: it emits a short descriptive bark ('plaque ') and then yields/suspends until the U8P_PLAQUENS::look interaction completes.
